input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the powertrain on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the powertrain on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. The scattershield is made from steel and assume the chain (not pictured) is centered on the gears. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule T.5.2.7c specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.5.2.7
            TITLE: chain drive - scatter shields for chains must:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.2.7: Chain Drive - Scatter shields for chains must:
T.5.2.7 Chain Drive - Scatter shields for chains must: 
a. Be made of 2.66 mm (0.105 inch) minimum thickness steel (no alternatives are allowed)
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 67 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
b. Have a minimum width equal to three times the width of the chain 
c. Be centered on the center line of the chain  
d. Stay aligned with the chain under all conditions

intermediate_steps.founded_objects: dimension arrow, dimension line, geometric symbol

final_answer:
Explanation:The rule T.5.2.7c requires the scatter shield to be centered on the center line of the chain. The engineering drawing does not provide an explicit dimension that measures the centering or offset of the scatter shield relative to the chain center line. According to the instructions, if a dimension is not explicitly shown, compliance with the rule is assumed. Visually, the design appears symmetrical around the central axis, supporting the assumption of compliance.

Answer:Yes
